title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: importer returns typed object:
code: gwas <- import_gwas(ggpop_extdata("gwas", "gcta.mlma"), type = "gcta") pca <- import_pca(   ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenvec"),   type = "gcta",   eigenval = ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenval"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) admix <- import_admix(   ggpop_extdata("admixture"),   type = "admixture",   ind = ggpop_extdata("snp", "finalsnp_ld.fam"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) stats <- import_stats(   ggpop_extdata("Population_genomics_statistics", "pixy"),   type = "pixy" ) ld_decay <- import_ld_decay(   ggpop_extdata("ld_decay", "PopLDdecay"),   type = "poplddecay" ) selscan_chr1 <- import_selection(   ggpop_extdata("selective_sweep", "selscan"),   ihs = "chr1.ihs.out.100bins.norm",   nsl = "chr1.nsl.out.100bins.norm",   xpehh = "chr1.xpehh.out.norm",   xpnsl = "chr1.xpnsl.out.norm",   type = "selscan" ) class(gwas) #> [1] "ggpop_gwas" "data.frame" class(pca) #> [1] "ggpop_pca"  "data.frame" class(admix) #> [1] "ggpop_admix" "data.frame" class(stats) #> [1] "ggpop_stats" "data.frame" class(ld_decay) #> [1] "ggpop_ld_decay" "data.frame" class(selscan_chr1) #> [1] "ggpop_selection" "data.frame"

title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: Every module two user-facing plotting paths. Use direct plot_*() function want reference plot immediately, use ggpop() plus module geom_*() want compose ggplot layers. direct path:  ggplot extension path:  pattern applies across modules:
code: gwas |>   plot_manha() gwas |>   ggpop() +   geom_manha() gwas |> ggpop() + ggpop::geom_qq() pca |> ggpop() + geom_pca() admix |> ggpop() + geom_admix(k = 3, order_group = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with ggpop
text: Use plot_manha() ggpop() + geom_manha() Manhattan plots. Use plot_qq() ggpop() + ggpop::geom_qq() Q-Q plots. Use plot_pca() ggpop() + geom_pca() PCA plots. Use plot_admix() ggpop() + geom_admix() admixture plots. Use plot_stats() ggpop() + geom_stats() windowed population statistics. Use plot_ld_decay() ggpop() + geom_ld_decay() LD decay summaries. Use plot_selection() ggpop() + geom_selection() selective sweep scans. Treat direct plot_*() functions reference style; geom_*() look inside ggplot composition. Use compatibility article need original pophelper workflows.

title: Admixture plots
code: admix <- import_admix(   ggpop_extdata("admixture"),   type = "admixture",   ind = ggpop_extdata("snp", "finalsnp_ld.fam"),   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt") ) plot_admix(admix, k = "all") plot_admix(admix, k = c(2, 4)) ggpop(admix) + geom_admix(k = 3)

title: Admixture plots
text: pop_group.txt uses two columns, sample pop. joined import: native ggplot implementation mirrors supported pophelper plotQ() behavior : individual labels show_sample_labels = TRUE; sorting individuals sort = "", sort = "label", cluster name K1; group labels show_group_labels = TRUE; sorting group labels using order_group = TRUE.  layered ggplot path returns kind ggplot object:
code: head(import_pop_group(ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt"))) #>   sample_id  pop #> 1      P001 PopC #> 2      P004 PopB #> 3      P006 PopC #> 4      P009 PopA #> 5      P010 PopB #> 6      P012 PopB plot_admix(   admix,   k = 3,   sort = "all",   order_group = TRUE,   show_group_labels = TRUE,   show_sample_labels = TRUE ) ggpop(admix) +   geom_admix(k = 3, sort = "all", order_group = TRUE)

title: Admixture plots
text: plot_admix() direct plot wrapper. geom_admix() layered interface want build larger ggplot object . routes use visual defaults; plot_admix() reference style, ggpop(admix) + geom_admix() reproduces publication-level look inside ggplot composition.
code: plot_admix(admix, k = 3) ggpop(admix) + geom_admix(k = 3)

title: LD decay
text: bundled example contains PopLDdecay final_ld.stat.gz result file. importer standardizes Dist dist, Mean_r^2 r2, NumberPairs n_pairs. Population grouping follows pop_group.txt convention used PCA admixture. LD decay file label stored sample_id; matching sample appears pop_group, corresponding pop value used colouring.
code: ld_dir <- ggpop_extdata("ld_decay", "PopLDdecay") ld_decay <- import_ld_decay(ld_dir, type = "poplddecay") class(ld_decay) #> [1] "ggpop_ld_decay" "data.frame" head(ld_decay) #>   dist     r2        pop  sample_id dist_kb n_pairs             file #> 1    5 0.7569 PopLDdecay PopLDdecay   0.005     401 final_ld.stat.gz #> 2    6 0.7733 PopLDdecay PopLDdecay   0.006     425 final_ld.stat.gz #> 3    7 0.7564 PopLDdecay PopLDdecay   0.007     379 final_ld.stat.gz #> 4    8 0.7497 PopLDdecay PopLDdecay   0.008     370 final_ld.stat.gz #> 5    9 0.7755 PopLDdecay PopLDdecay   0.009     338 final_ld.stat.gz #> 6   10 0.7513 PopLDdecay PopLDdecay   0.010     344 final_ld.stat.gz #>       source     .group #> 1 poplddecay PopLDdecay #> 2 poplddecay PopLDdecay #> 3 poplddecay PopLDdecay #> 4 poplddecay PopLDdecay #> 5 poplddecay PopLDdecay #> 6 poplddecay PopLDdecay ld_grouped <- import_ld_decay(   ld_dir,   pop = "P001",   pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt"),   type = "poplddecay" ) unique(ld_grouped$pop) #> [1] "PopC"

title: Selective sweep scans
text: bundled selscan example contains normalized iHS, nSL, iHH12, XP-EHH, XP-nSL outputs. Directory imports auto-discover supported result files. can also import selected files explicitly. Relative paths resolved inside dir.
code: selscan_dir <- ggpop_extdata("selective_sweep", "selscan") selscan <- import_selection(selscan_dir, type = "selscan") class(selscan) #> [1] "ggpop_selection" "data.frame" unique(selscan$stat) #> [1] "ihh12" "ihs"   "nsl"   "xpehh" "xpnsl" selscan_chr1 <- import_selection(   selscan_dir,   ihs = "chr1.ihs.out.100bins.norm",   nsl = "chr1.nsl.out.100bins.norm",   xpehh = "chr1.xpehh.out.norm",   xpnsl = "chr1.xpnsl.out.norm",   type = "selscan" ) unique(selscan_chr1$stat) #> [1] "ihh12" "ihs"   "nsl"   "xpehh" "xpnsl"

title: Selective sweep scans
text: plot_selection() stacks selected statistics vertically keeps genomic position aligned x-axis. Region filters work points selscan overlapping windows XPCLR. default, genome-wide calls use Manhattan-like chromosome axis. Calls chr, start, end use single-region style, position shown directly megabases.  layered API follows grammar modules:  Use value = "absolute" direction statistic less important magnitude. Quantile thresholds computed statistic, chromosome, region filters applied.
code: plot_selection(   selscan_chr1,   stat = c("ihs", "nsl", "xpehh", "xpnsl"),   chr = "1" ) selscan_chr1 |>   ggpop() +   geom_selection(stat = "ihs", chr = "1", threshold = 2) plot_selection(   selscan_chr1,   stat = "ihs",   chr = "1",   value = "absolute",   threshold = 0.95,   threshold_type = "quantile" )

title: Selective sweep scans
text: genome-wide scan summaries, often useful lay chromosomes end end like Manhattan plot. Fine-scale structure compressed, view good seeing chromosome-scale shifts scan outliers across genome. Show absolute iHS scale:  Plot normalized nSL, XP-EHH, XP-nSL z-score-like panels:
code: selscan_selected <- import_selection(   selscan_dir,   ihs1 = "chr1.ihs.out.100bins.norm",   ihs2 = "chr2.ihs.out.100bins.norm",   ihs3 = "chr3.ihs.out.100bins.norm",   nsl1 = "chr1.nsl.out.100bins.norm",   nsl2 = "chr2.nsl.out.100bins.norm",   nsl3 = "chr3.nsl.out.100bins.norm",   xpehh1 = "chr1.xpehh.out.norm",   xpehh2 = "chr2.xpehh.out.norm",   xpehh3 = "chr3.xpehh.out.norm",   xpnsl1 = "chr1.xpnsl.out.norm",   xpnsl2 = "chr2.xpnsl.out.norm",   xpnsl3 = "chr3.xpnsl.out.norm",   type = "selscan" )  finite_scan <- selscan_selected[is.finite(selscan_selected$value), ] plot_selection(   finite_scan,   stat = "ihs",   style = "manhattan",   value = "absolute",   threshold = 0.95,   threshold_type = "quantile" ) plot_selection(   finite_scan,   stat = c("nsl", "xpehh", "xpnsl"),   style = "manhattan" )

title: Selective sweep scans
text: XPCLR outputs window-based. importer keeps original start end columns, computes pos midpoint, prefers xpclr_norm available.  Use geom_selection() scan composed additional ggplot layers annotations.
code: xpclr_dir <- ggpop_extdata("selective_sweep", "xpclr") xpclr <- import_selection(xpclr_dir, type = "xpclr") class(xpclr) #> [1] "ggpop_selection" "data.frame" unique(xpclr$stat) #> [1] "xpclr" head(xpclr[c("chr", "start", "end", "pos", "value")]) #>                           chr  start   end      pos      value #> xpclr_allchr_merged.tsv.1   1      1 5e+05 250000.5 -0.1051471 #> xpclr_allchr_merged.tsv.2   1 100001 6e+05 350000.5 -0.1051471 #> xpclr_allchr_merged.tsv.3   1 200001 7e+05 450000.5 -0.1051471 #> xpclr_allchr_merged.tsv.4   1 300001 8e+05 550000.5 -0.1051471 #> xpclr_allchr_merged.tsv.5   1 400001 9e+05 650000.5 -0.1051471 #> xpclr_allchr_merged.tsv.6   1 500001 1e+06 750000.5 -0.1051471 plot_selection(   xpclr,   stat = "xpclr",   style = "manhattan",   threshold = 0.95,   threshold_type = "quantile" )

title: Population genomics statistics
text: bundled pixy example dense chr2L windowed data set. contains 4,000 tidy rows across five statistics: 500 pairwise Dxy windows, 500 pairwise FST windows, 1,000 within-population windows pi, Tajima’s D, Watterson’s theta. Windows 100 bp wide span positions 1–50,000. can also import selected files explicitly:
code: pixy_dir <- ggpop_extdata("Population_genomics_statistics", "pixy") stats <- import_stats(pixy_dir, type = "pixy") class(stats) #> [1] "ggpop_stats" "data.frame" unique(stats$stat) #> [1] "dxy"             "fst"             "pi"              #> [4] "tajima_d"        "watterson_theta" range(stats$start) #> [1]     1 49901 selected <- import_stats(   pixy_dir,   pi = "pixy_pi.txt",   fst = "pixy_fst.txt",   tajima = "pixy_tajima_d.txt",   type = "pixy" ) unique(selected$stat) #> [1] "dxy"             "fst"             "pi"              #> [4] "tajima_d"        "watterson_theta"

title: Population genomics statistics
text: vcftools results use different column naming convention, enter typed plotting object. Supported examples include .windowed.pi, .windowed.weir.fst, .Tajima.D files. Explicit file selection works way:
code: vcftools_dir <- ggpop_extdata("Population_genomics_statistics", "vcftools") vcftools_stats <- import_stats(vcftools_dir, type = "vcftools") class(vcftools_stats) #> [1] "ggpop_stats" "data.frame" unique(vcftools_stats$stat) #> [1] "tajima_d" "pi"       "fst" vcftools_selected <- import_stats(   vcftools_dir,   pi = "vcftools.windowed.pi",   fst = "vcftools.windowed.weir.fst",   tajima = "vcftools.Tajima.D",   type = "vcftools" ) unique(vcftools_selected$stat) #> [1] "tajima_d" "pi"       "fst"

title: ggplot2 Extensions for Population Genetics Plots
text: goal ggpop streamline publication-ready population-genomics visualization R. combines typed import helpers, direct plotting functions, composable ggplot2 extension layers GWAS, PCA, admixture results. also includes population genomics statistics module windowed FST, pi, Tajima’s D, Dxy, Watterson’s theta summaries, LD decay curves, plus selective sweep scan plots selscan XPCLR outputs. ggpop focuses tidy workflow: paths return ggplot objects. direct plot_*() functions define publication-style visual contract, matching geom_*() layers follow defaults inside ggpop() pipeline. GWAS Manhattan plots support explicit palette control:
code: import_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") |>   plot_manha()  import_gwas("assoc.mlma", type = "gcta") |>   ggpop() +   geom_manha() plot_manha(gwas, palette = "publication") plot_manha(gwas, palette = c("#4E79A7", "#F28E2B"), binary = TRUE) plot_manha(gwas, threshold_color = "#E15759", suggestive_color = "#4E79A7")

title: ggpop manuscript draft: figure-driven positioning
text: tidypopgen 已经提出了 population genetics 的 tidy grammar，但它主要解决的是 SNP genotype 数据结构、磁盘存储、分块计算、数据操作和群体遗传分析问题。ggpop 的定位不同：它不试图替代 tidypopgen，也不把所有上游计算都搬进 R。对于现代大规模测序数据，PCA、admixture、GWAS 和 window statistics 等核心计算通常更适合由 PLINK、GCTA、ADMIXTURE、GEMMA、EMMAX、pixy、VCFtools 等成熟工具完成。 目前缺少的是统一的群体基因组学可视化层。研究者经常需要在 qqman、pophelper、自写 ggplot2 脚本和不同工具输出格式之间切换，导致接口割裂、配色不统一、主题不一致、代码难复用、图形难以达到统一的发表风格。ggpop 的价值是补上最后一公里：把不同来源的结果导入为 typed S3 tidy objects，再通过统一的 plot_*() 和 ggpop() + geom_*() 语法生成标准 ggplot 图形。 可以将 ggpop 概括为：面向群体基因组学结果的 tidy ggplot2 可视化扩展。它借鉴 tidyplots 的用户友好思想，但服务于群体基因组学场景；它借鉴 fastman 的高效 GWAS 绘图逻辑，改善大型关联结果的 Manhattan 和 Q-Q 图绘制体验；它把常用的 pophelper admixture 绘图思想改写为 ggplot2 对象，使用户既保留熟悉的 K 选择、排序和分组标签，又获得 ggplot 图层组合能力。
